knownIssues.title: Tobii eye tracking requires per-user recalibration
knownIssues.description: The Tobii eye tracking system needs to be recalibrated for each user. Glasses wearers or users with astigmatism may find calibration less accurate.
knownIssues.workaround: Recalibrate eye tracking in SteamVR settings before each session if you share the headset. Use the Tobii Eye Tracking Core Software for advanced calibration options.

knownIssues.title: Foveated rendering requires game-side support
knownIssues.description: Eye tracking-driven foveated rendering only works in titles that explicitly support it via the Tobii SDK or SteamVR Eye Tracking API. Most games do not benefit automatically.
knownIssues.workaround: Check each game's documentation for eye tracking / foveated rendering support. Standard performance optimization tips still apply for unsupported titles.

knownIssues.title: Link Box can cause intermittent disconnects
knownIssues.description: The HTC Link Box that converts the headset tether to DisplayPort and USB is a known failure point. It can cause random disconnects and display issues.
knownIssues.workaround: Ensure Link Box firmware is up to date via SteamVR. Replace the Link Box if disconnects persist.

optimizationTips.tip: Enable eye-tracked foveated rendering in supported titles
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: In games that support it, the eye tracking makes your GPU only render in full detail where you're actually looking — big performance savings.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Tobii-based dynamic foveated rendering reduces GPU load by rendering the peripheral region at 50-70% resolution while keeping the foveal region at 100%. In supported titles this can yield 20-40% frame time reduction. Enable via each game's eye tracking settings or the Tobii Eye Tracking Core software.

optimizationTips.tip: Supersampling at 1.3x sharpens Fresnel lens aliasing
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Turning up picture quality in SteamVR removes the jagged edges that can be visible through the Fresnel lenses.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: The Vive Pro Eye uses the same Fresnel optics as the Vive Pro. Aliasing is visible at 1.0x SS through these lenses. 1.3x supersampling in SteamVR provides a clear sharpness improvement with manageable GPU overhead on GTX 1080-class hardware.

optimizationTips.tip: Calibrate eye tracking before performance-critical sessions
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Accurate eye tracking calibration makes sure foveated rendering focuses detail exactly where you're looking.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Poorly calibrated eye tracking shifts the high-resolution foveal region away from the actual gaze point, producing blurring at fixation. Recalibrate using the 5-point calibration in SteamVR → Eye Tracking. Consider the 9-point extended calibration for users with significant astigmatism.
